iterations.summary: Infrastructure + 1 fresh trace. Resumable state machine in place.
Allocation decision (Option A: Etingof-only, 12+6) and 2 more traces.
deliverables: data/allocation.json — 12 trace + 6 eval = 18 problems, manually curated from 24-problem usable pool
Haiku-tagging attempt (output unreliable, manually re-curated; lessons noted)
2 fresh traces: ex_5_18 (group Euler char), ex_9_13 (Amitsur–Levitzki)
3 Structs: ex_2_20, ex_5_18, ex_9_13 (Haiku subagent, anti-leakage prompt)
Decontamination verified: all 3 iter-0 + all 3 new eval problems pass against current 6 traces
lessons: Haiku judgeability tagger hallucinated — claimed 'self_contained: yes' for 'Check this' / 'Do this in detail' problems; pulled technique tags from chapter context not problem text. At N=35 manual curation is faster than another tagger round.

Pool ceiling: 24 cleanly usable problems (judgeable + self-contained + no answer leak). 12+6 split chosen as conservative allocation; could push to 18+6 by including borderline-context problems with prompt fixups.

eval.note: iter0 partial: 3 problems x 2 conditions x 1 sample = 6 datapoints. Full eval target: 6 problems x 2 conditions x 4 samples = 48 datapoints.

iter3_summary.interpretation: Both judges put |delta| ~ 0.01, in the noise floor. Reversal from iter-0 (delta = -0.15) attributable to methodological fixes: zero transposition failures observed (vs dominant in iter-0), zero single-template anchoring failures observed. H1 (>=10pp lift) falsified; H2 (no off-domain harm) supported.

planned: Generate ex_7_27 trace (matched to eval ex_7_28) — diagnostic test of whether RAG ex_7_28 win persists when matched trace exists
Generate ex_4_9, ex_4_15, ex_2_12 traces toward target 10-12
Re-run inference round (8 subagents x 6 problems = 48 outputs) with expanded corpus
Re-judge with both Opus and Sonnet
If aggregate stays |delta| < 0.05, declare experiment closed
